pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **Look, I understand why "Ghost Pepper" once drafted Haiti in the fourth round—it's endearing, really, in the way that getting your lunch money stolen is nostalgic—but it does cement Devin firmly in second place.** I get that executing a game plan that includes Bosnia and South Africa might have seemed like a hidden gem of a strategy, but when you're up against someone like "Bubba G" who is literally counting on France to make him look like a genius at every turn, you start to see the cracks in Devin's plan. Meanwhile, "The Noisemaker" Rafe is doing his best impression of a soccer savant with picks like Germany and Mexico, but bless his heart, he's still grasping at the bottom rung of the ladder. But hey, unpredictability is the essence of youth, and I’m sure he’s learning a lot about heartbreak in the process. As we look forward, you can bet with absurd confidence that Rafe’s next matches will probably continue this educational experience—because stumbling is part of growing up.

pundit_takes.headline: Bubba G Sets New Fantasy Record With Congo's Tactical Masterclass
pundit_takes.subtitle: Three points cement Gunner's lead; Congo overachieves under Inglewood sun.

pundit_takes.headline: Ghost Pepper Inches Closer To Top As USA Enjoys Day Off
pundit_takes.subtitle: Devin's strategic allocation of rest days keeps him in second.

pundit_takes.headline: Mustard Boy's Austrian Ambitions Sputter Against Algeria's Unexpected Steel Wall
pundit_takes.subtitle: A shared point lets Zach savor the ketchup of fourth place.

pundit_takes.headline: Backpass Assassin Misses Clean Sheet Target As Jordan Falls Short
pundit_takes.subtitle: Gayden's tactical misfires against Argentina threaten Championship glory.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe's 12% win chance is higher than his GPA—still in it, folks!
Rafe's Tier-1 pick Germany has ghosted harder than his middle school crush.
Rafe and Gayden trading punches? Call Maury, someone's getting disowned before the playoffs.
Gunner leads with the model's blessing—looks like Rafe cast a teenage hex.
